seoDescription: Browse P-Duke DC-DC converters, AC-DC power modules, and railway-certified power supplies. Technical specifications, selection guides, and FAE support from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What types of power products does P-Duke offer?
faqs.answer: P-Duke offers a comprehensive range of power conversion products including DC-DC converters with ultra-wide input ranges, AC-DC power modules for embedded applications, railway-certified power supplies meeting EN 50155, and medical-grade power modules with 2xMOPP isolation. Each product category includes multiple series optimized for specific applications such as industrial automation, transportation, medical equipment, and telecommunications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our product categories to find the right P-Duke power solution for your application, or contact our FAE team for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I choose between DC-DC and AC-DC power products?
faqs.answer: DC-DC converters are used when you have a DC voltage source (such as a battery or another power supply) and need to convert it to a different DC voltage level. They are ideal for distributed power systems, battery-powered devices, and on-board voltage conversion. AC-DC power modules are used when your input is AC mains voltage and you need to convert it to DC for your equipment. P-Duke offers both types with various input/output combinations to meet your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to discuss your input power source and output requirements for guidance on selecting the appropriate power product type.

faqs.question: What certifications do P-Duke power products have?
faqs.answer: P-Duke power products carry comprehensive certifications for various industries: Railway products are certified to EN 50155, EN 61373, and EN 45545-2 for rolling stock applications; Medical products meet IEC 60601-1 with 2xMOPP isolation for patient safety; Industrial products comply with IEC 62368-1 and UL safety standards; Military products meet MIL-STD-810 environmental requirements. All products are RoHS compliant and many are REACH compliant for environmental safety.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify the specific certification requirements for your application and target markets, then select P-Duke models with appropriate certifications.

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency of P-Duke converters?
faqs.answer: P-Duke converters offer high efficiency across their product range. DC-DC converters typically achieve 87-92% efficiency depending on the series and operating conditions. AC-DC power modules achieve 85-90% efficiency. The high efficiency minimizes heat generation, allowing operation in high ambient temperatures and reducing cooling requirements. P-Duke uses synchronous rectification and optimized magnetic designs to achieve these efficiency levels while maintaining high reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: For thermally constrained applications or battery-powered systems, prioritize high-efficiency P-Duke series to minimize power loss and heat generation.

faqs.question: Does P-Duke offer custom power solutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, P-Duke offers custom and modified standard power solutions for applications with specific requirements. Customization options include: Special input/output voltage combinations; Modified temperature ranges and environmental specifications; Additional screening and burn-in procedures; Custom mechanical configurations; Special isolation requirements. Custom solutions require minimum order quantities and longer lead times but provide optimized performance for critical applications. Contact our sales team to discuss your custom requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team to discuss your custom power supply requirements and explore available customization options with P-Duke.

categories.name: DC-DC Converters
categories.description: High-reliability DC-DC converters with ultra-wide input ranges for industrial and railway applications
categories.longDescription: P-Duke DC-DC converters provide reliable voltage conversion for distributed power systems, battery-powered equipment, and industrial control applications. Featuring ultra-wide input ranges up to 14:1, these converters accommodate varying source voltages from battery systems or unregulated power sources. Available in isolated and non-isolated configurations with power ratings from 1W to 300W, P-Duke converters offer high efficiency, compact size, and exceptional reliability. All converters undergo 100% burn-in testing and feature comprehensive protection against overvoltage, overcurrent, and overtemperature conditions.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select P-Duke DC-DC Converters
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting DC-DC converters based on input range, isolation requirements, and application needs

faqs.question: What is the advantage of ultra-wide input range converters?
faqs.answer: Ultra-wide input range converters (typically 4:1 or 14:1) provide significant advantages in applications with varying input voltages: Battery-powered systems where voltage drops during discharge; Railway applications with varying battery voltages; Systems powered from unregulated sources; Applications requiring compatibility with multiple input standards. A single wide-input converter can replace multiple narrow-input models, reducing inventory and simplifying design. For example, a 9-36V input converter handles both 12V and 24V systems, while a 14-160V converter covers all standard battery voltages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select ultra-wide input converters for battery-powered or variable input applications to maximize flexibility and minimize inventory.

faqs.question: How do I select the right isolation voltage for my converter?
faqs.answer: Isolation voltage selection depends on your application requirements: Functional isolation (1000-1500V) is suitable for breaking ground loops and providing basic isolation in industrial equipment; Reinforced isolation (3000V) is required for medical equipment and safety-critical applications where operator protection is needed; Working voltage ratings must also be considered for continuous operation. For railway applications, EN 50155 specifies isolation requirements. For medical applications, IEC 60601-1 requires 2xMOPP which typically translates to 4000V test voltage. Always verify the specific requirements of your application safety standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: For medical or safety-critical applications, select converters with 3000V or higher isolation. For industrial ground loop breaking, 1500V is typically adequate.

faqs.question: What is the typical MTBF of P-Duke converters?
faqs.answer: P-Duke converters are designed for high reliability with MTBF ratings typically exceeding 1 million hours at 25°C ambient temperature. The high reliability is achieved through: Conservative component derating; High-quality components from qualified suppliers; 100% burn-in testing to eliminate early failures; Comprehensive protection circuits; Robust thermal design. MTBF is calculated per MIL-HDBK-217 or Telcordia SR-332 methodologies. Actual field reliability depends on operating conditions including temperature, electrical stress, and environmental factors. For critical applications, P-Duke can provide detailed reliability analysis and accelerated life testing data.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-reliability applications, request detailed MTBF calculations and reliability data for your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: Can P-Duke converters operate in parallel for higher power?
faqs.answer: Many P-Duke DC-DC converters support parallel operation for increased output current or redundancy. There are several approaches: Passive droop sharing where converters with similar output voltages naturally share current; Active current sharing using dedicated control pins for precise load balancing; External OR-ing diodes for redundancy applications to prevent back-feeding. When paralleling converters, ensure all units have matched output voltages and adequate thermal management. Not all models support parallel operation - check the datasheet for specifications. For high-reliability applications, consider using N+1 redundancy with external OR-ing diodes.
faqs.decisionGuide: For parallel operation, select converters specifically designed for current sharing and follow P-Duke application notes for proper implementation.

faqs.question: What protection features are included in P-Duke converters?
faqs.answer: P-Duke converters include comprehensive protection features: Input undervoltage lockout (UVLO) prevents operation when input voltage is too low; Output overcurrent protection (OCP) limits current during overload or short circuit; Output overvoltage protection (OVP) prevents damage to sensitive loads; Overtemperature protection (OTP) shuts down the converter if internal temperature exceeds safe limits; Short circuit protection allows continuous operation into a short with automatic recovery. Many converters also feature remote on/off control and output voltage trim. These protection features ensure reliable operation and protect both the converter and the load from damage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the specific protection features in the datasheet to ensure they meet your application's safety and reliability requirements.

faqs.question: What is the minimum input voltage for proper operation?
faqs.answer: The EC7AW24-72S12 has a specified input range of 24-72V DC, but includes undervoltage lockout (UVLO) protection. The typical UVLO threshold is 21V with 2V hysteresis, meaning the converter will shut down if input drops below approximately 21V and will not restart until input rises above 23V. This protection prevents abnormal operation during brownout conditions or when starting from a slowly rising input voltage. For reliable operation, ensure your input voltage source can maintain at least 24V under all load conditions including startup inrush. If using the converter with a 24V battery, account for voltage sag during discharge - a nominal 24V lead-acid battery can drop to 21V at 50% discharge, which is still within the operating range.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your input voltage source maintains at least 24V under all operating conditions including startup and peak loads.

faqs.question: How much output capacitance can the EC7AW24-72S12 drive?
faqs.answer: The EC7AW24-72S12 can drive up to 1000uF of external output capacitance without instability. The converter includes internal output capacitors for basic filtering, but additional external capacitance is often needed for: Reducing output ripple voltage under transient loads; Providing hold-up time during input interruptions; Filtering high-frequency noise for sensitive loads. When adding external capacitors, use low-ESR ceramic or tantalum capacitors placed close to the load. Very large capacitance values (>1000uF) can cause startup issues as the converter must charge the capacitor while maintaining regulation. If large capacitance is required, consider using a soft-start circuit or current-limited precharge. For typical applications, 100-470uF is sufficient for most loads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Limit external output capacitance to 1000uF maximum. For larger capacitance needs, consult our FAE team for proper implementation.

faqs.question: What is the isolation test voltage for the EC7AW24-72S12?
faqs.answer: The EC7AW24-72S12 is rated for 3000V DC isolation between input and output, tested for 60 seconds during production. This is a dielectric withstand test voltage, not the continuous working voltage. The continuous working isolation voltage is typically 500-1000V depending on environmental conditions and safety agency requirements. The isolation barrier is designed to meet reinforced insulation requirements per IEC 60950-1 and IEC 62368-1. The isolation resistance is greater than 10^9 ohms at 500V DC. For medical applications requiring 2xMOPP (Means of Patient Protection), verify that the specific model meets the required creepage and clearance distances for your application. The 3000V rating provides substantial margin for industrial applications with transient overvoltages.
faqs.decisionGuide: For safety-critical applications, verify that the isolation rating meets your specific safety standard requirements.

faqs.question: Can the EC7AW24-72S12 be used in parallel with other converters?
faqs.answer: The EC7AW24-72S12 does not include active current sharing and is not recommended for direct parallel connection without external circuitry. For increased power requirements, consider these approaches: Use a single higher-power converter from the EC15AW series (15W); Implement external OR-ing diodes with current balancing resistors for passive sharing; Use the EC7AW24-72S12 with different output voltages to create multiple isolated rails. If parallel operation is required, external Schottky OR-ing diodes should be used to prevent back-feeding, and small series resistors (0.1-0.5 ohm) can help balance current. However, this approach reduces efficiency and regulation accuracy. For new designs requiring more than 7W, I recommend selecting a single higher-power converter rather than paralleling multiple units.
faqs.decisionGuide: For power requirements above 7W, select a single higher-power converter rather than attempting to parallel EC7AW units.

faqs.question: What is the thermal derating for the EC7AW24-72S12?
faqs.answer: The EC7AW24-72S12 is rated for full 7W output from -40°C to +71°C ambient with natural convection cooling. Above 71°C, output power must be derated linearly to 50% at 85°C. The derating curve is based on the converter's internal temperature rise and maximum allowable junction temperatures of the semiconductors. For example, at 78°C ambient, maximum output power is approximately 5.25W (75% of rated). The converter's efficiency decreases slightly at higher temperatures, increasing internal heat generation. For high-temperature applications, consider: Improving airflow around the converter; Reducing load power; Selecting a higher-power converter and operating it at partial load; Using thermal vias and copper pours on the PCB to spread heat. The converter includes overtemperature protection that shuts down at approximately 115°C internal temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: For ambient temperatures above 71°C, calculate required derating or improve thermal management to maintain reliable operation.

faqs.question: What are the EMI characteristics of the EC7AW24-72S12?
faqs.answer: The EC7AW24-72S12 is designed to meet EN 55032 Class B emissions standards with appropriate external filtering. The converter uses a switching frequency of approximately 300kHz, which is high enough to allow small magnetic components while being low enough to minimize EMI. The metal package provides shielding that reduces radiated emissions. For conducted emissions compliance: Add input filtering capacitors (typically 10-100uF electrolytic plus 0.1uF ceramic); Consider a common-mode choke for applications with strict EMI requirements; Follow PCB layout guidelines in the application note. The 3000V isolation transformer provides inherent common-mode noise rejection. For medical applications with very low leakage current requirements, additional isolation may be needed. P-Duke provides application notes with recommended filter designs for various EMI standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: For EMI-sensitive applications or strict regulatory requirements, implement recommended input filtering and follow PCB layout guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the typical efficiency at light loads?
faqs.answer: The EC4AW24-72S05 maintains good efficiency across the load range: At 100% load (800mA): 87% typical efficiency; At 75% load (600mA): 86% typical efficiency; At 50% load (400mA): 84% typical efficiency; At 25% load (200mA): 80% typical efficiency. The efficiency curve is optimized for industrial applications where the converter typically operates at moderate to full load. At very light loads (<10%), efficiency decreases as the converter's internal power consumption becomes significant relative to output power. For battery-powered applications where light-load efficiency is critical, consider P-Duke's low-power series which are optimized for extended battery life.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications with varying load conditions, verify efficiency at your typical operating points.

faqs.question: What is the output ripple voltage of the EC4AW24-72S05?
faqs.answer: The EC4AW24-72S05 features low output ripple voltage: Typical ripple is 50mV peak-to-peak at full load; Ripple decreases proportionally with load current; Maximum ripple is 100mV peak-to-peak under worst-case conditions. The low ripple is achieved through optimized output filtering and switching frequency selection. For applications requiring even lower ripple: Add external ceramic capacitors (10-100uF) close to the load; Use a small LC filter if ripple requirements are stringent; Consider linear post-regulation for noise-sensitive analog circuits. The converter's switching frequency (typically 300kHz) is high enough to be easily filtered while maintaining good efficiency. Ripple measurements should be made with a oscilloscope probe directly at the output terminals with proper grounding technique.
faqs.decisionGuide: For noise-sensitive applications, add external filtering or consider linear post-regulation.

faqs.question: Can the EC4AW24-72S05 be used in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: The EC4AW24-72S05 can be used in some automotive applications with considerations: Input voltage range (24-72V) covers 24V commercial vehicle systems; Operating temperature range (-40°C to +85°C) meets automotive requirements; However, the converter is not specifically qualified to automotive standards (AEC-Q100); Load dump protection may be needed for 24V vehicle systems where voltage transients can exceed 100V; Crank voltage drop immunity should be verified for the specific application. For automotive-qualified DC-DC converters, P-Duke offers specific series designed and tested to automotive standards. For commercial vehicles (trucks, buses, construction equipment) where automotive qualification is not strictly required, the EC4AW series can be suitable with appropriate external protection.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, verify voltage transient requirements and consider automotive-qualified converters.

faqs.question: What is the startup time of the EC4AW24-72S05?
faqs.answer: The EC4AW24-72S05 features fast startup for responsive operation: Typical startup time is 50ms from input voltage application to regulated output; This includes internal soft-start to limit inrush current; Output rise time is controlled to prevent overshoot; Startup time is relatively constant across the input voltage range. The fast startup is beneficial for: Systems requiring quick power-up after input application; Battery-powered devices where power is cycled frequently; Applications with supervisory circuits requiring fast power good indication. The converter includes internal soft-start circuitry that gradually ramps the output voltage, eliminating inrush current spikes that could stress input capacitors or cause EMI issues. For applications requiring even faster startup, contact P-Duke for specific recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For time-critical applications, verify startup time meets your system requirements.

faqs.question: How does the converter handle short circuit conditions?
faqs.answer: The EC4AW24-72S05 includes comprehensive short circuit protection: Hiccup mode operation - the converter shuts down and periodically attempts restart during sustained short circuit; Automatic recovery - normal operation resumes when short circuit is removed; Current limiting - peak current is limited to protect internal components; Thermal protection - converter shuts down if internal temperature exceeds limits during fault. Short circuit behavior: Output current is limited to approximately 1.5A during short; Converter enters hiccup mode if short persists for more than 100ms; Restart attempts occur every 500ms; No damage occurs from indefinite short circuit. This protection scheme protects both the converter and the wiring from damage during fault conditions. The hiccup mode prevents excessive heating while providing automatic recovery when the fault is cleared.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications with potential output shorts, the built-in protection ensures safe operation and automatic recovery.

faqs.question: What is the load regulation of the EC4AW24-72S05?
faqs.answer: The EC4AW24-72S05 provides excellent load regulation to maintain stable output voltage under varying load conditions: Typical load regulation is +/-0.5% from no load to full load; This means the 5V output varies by less than 25mV across the entire load range; Line regulation (input voltage variation) is typically +/-0.2%; Combined regulation is better than +/-1% under all operating conditions. The tight regulation is achieved through feedback control that continuously adjusts the switching to maintain output voltage. For applications requiring even tighter regulation: Add a linear post-regulator for the most sensitive circuits; Use remote sense if available on the specific model; Ensure adequate input capacitance to prevent input voltage droop under load transients. The excellent regulation makes this converter suitable for powering sensitive digital circuits and analog references.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring extremely tight regulation, consider adding linear post-regulation for critical circuits.

faqs.question: What is the advantage of universal input AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: Universal input AC-DC modules (85-264VAC) provide significant advantages for global applications: Single SKU works worldwide without voltage selection switches; Eliminates inventory complexity for international products; Automatically adapts to 115V or 230V mains; Simplifies product design and certification; Reduces field support issues from incorrect voltage settings. The wide input range is achieved through active power factor correction and wide-range switching power supply topologies. For industrial equipment exported globally, universal input modules eliminate the need for region-specific variants and reduce total cost of ownership.
faqs.decisionGuide: For products sold globally or in multiple regions, universal input modules simplify design and reduce inventory complexity.

faqs.question: What safety certifications do P-Duke AC-DC modules have?
faqs.answer: P-Duke AC-DC modules carry comprehensive safety certifications: UL/cUL 62368-1 for North American information technology equipment; EN 62368-1 for European safety compliance; CB scheme for international certification acceptance; IEC 60950-1 for legacy equipment compatibility; EN 60335-1 for household appliances; Medical versions certified to IEC 60601-1 with 2xMOPP. All certifications include complete test reports and factory surveillance. The certifications cover both PCB mount and chassis mount configurations. For specific market requirements, P-Duke can provide additional certifications such as CCC for China or PSE for Japan.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify the specific safety certifications required for your target markets and select modules with appropriate approvals.

faqs.question: What is the typical hold-up time for P-Duke AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: P-Duke AC-DC modules provide hold-up time to maintain output during brief input interruptions: Typical hold-up time is 20ms at full load and nominal input voltage; This meets the standard requirement for industrial equipment (typically 10-20ms); Hold-up time increases at lighter loads and higher input voltages; For extended hold-up requirements, external bulk capacitors can be added. The hold-up time is determined by the internal energy storage capacitors and converter efficiency. For applications requiring longer ride-through (such as industrial controls), consider modules with extended hold-up options or add external hold-up capacitors sized for your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring extended hold-up time, select modules with extended hold-up options or add external capacitors.

faqs.question: How do I meet EMI requirements with AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: P-Duke AC-DC modules include internal EMI filtering and are designed to meet EN 55032 Class B with appropriate external components: Internal common-mode and differential-mode filtering reduces conducted emissions; Shielded transformers minimize radiated emissions; Recommended external filter components are specified in the datasheet; PCB layout guidelines help minimize EMI coupling. For Class B compliance, typical external filtering includes: Input X-capacitors (0.1-0.47uF); Common-mode choke (10-30mH); Y-capacitors for high-frequency noise; Proper earth grounding. P-Duke provides application notes with recommended filter designs for various EMI standards. Pre-compliance testing is recommended to verify meeting specific regional requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow P-Duke application notes for EMI filtering and PCB layout to ensure compliance with regional EMI standards.

faqs.question: What is the power factor of P-Duke AC-DC modules?
faqs.answer: P-Duke AC-DC modules feature active power factor correction (PFC) for high power factor and low harmonic distortion: Power factor is typically greater than 0.95 at full load; THD (Total Harmonic Distortion) is less than 15% at nominal input; Meets EN 61000-3-2 Class D harmonic current limits; Benefits include reduced input current, lower distribution losses, and compliance with regulations. The active PFC circuit shapes the input current to follow the voltage waveform, maximizing real power transfer. For higher power modules (>75W), active PFC is essential for regulatory compliance. Lower power modules may use passive PFC or valley-fill circuits to achieve acceptable power factor at lower cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring power factor correction or low harmonic distortion, select modules with active PFC.

faqs.question: What is the inrush current of the LPS60-24?
faqs.answer: The LPS60-24 has controlled inrush current to prevent nuisance tripping of circuit breakers: Typical inrush current is 30A peak for less than 5ms at 230VAC cold start; Inrush is lower at 115VAC (approximately 20A peak); The inrush current is limited by internal NTC thermistors and soft-start circuitry; Multiple modules can typically be powered from a single circuit breaker without coordination issues. For applications with very strict inrush limits, external inrush current limiters can be added. The inrush specification assumes startup at maximum input voltage with fully discharged input capacitors. Warm start (restarting immediately after shutdown) may have lower inrush due to residual charge on capacitors.
faqs.decisionGuide: For multiple modules on a single circuit, verify total inrush current against breaker trip curves.

faqs.question: What is the minimum load requirement for the LPS60-24?
faqs.answer: The LPS60-24 requires a minimum load for proper regulation: Minimum load is 10% of rated output (250mA at 24V); Operating below minimum load may result in output voltage rise or instability; For applications with light or no-load conditions, add a load resistor or select a lower power module; The minimum load ensures the feedback loop operates in its designed range. For applications with variable loads down to zero, consider: Adding a 2.4kohm (0.25W) load resistor to maintain minimum load; Using a module designed for zero-load operation; Implementing a load switch that maintains minimum load when main load is disconnected. The datasheet specifies minimum load requirements for each output voltage variant.
faqs.decisionGuide: For light load applications, verify minimum load requirements or add a load resistor to maintain regulation.

faqs.question: Can the LPS60-24 be used in parallel for higher power?
faqs.answer: The LPS60-24 does not support direct parallel connection for current sharing. For higher power requirements, consider these alternatives: Use a single higher-power module from the MPS series (100W or 150W); Implement external OR-ing diodes for redundancy (not current sharing); Use multiple modules with separate loads rather than paralleled outputs. If redundancy is required for critical applications: Use two LPS60-24 modules with external Schottky OR-ing diodes; Each module operates independently with diode isolation; This provides N+1 redundancy but not increased power capacity. For new designs requiring more than 60W, I recommend selecting the appropriate single module from the MPS series rather than attempting to parallel LPS60 units.
faqs.decisionGuide: For power requirements above 60W, select a single higher-power MPS series module rather than paralleling LPS60 units.

faqs.question: What is the isolation voltage of the LPS60-24?
faqs.answer: The LPS60-24 provides reinforced isolation between input and output: Isolation test voltage is 4000V AC for 60 seconds; Working voltage is 300V AC continuous; Reinforced insulation rating meets IEC 62368-1 requirements; Isolation resistance exceeds 100M ohms at 500V DC. The reinforced isolation provides safety protection equivalent to double insulation, making it suitable for: Equipment with accessible conductive parts; Medical equipment (when combined with appropriate creepage/clearance); Industrial controls requiring high reliability. The isolation barrier is tested 100% in production and designed for long-term reliability. For medical applications, verify that the specific model meets the required 2xMOPP (Means of Patient Protection) requirements for your application class.
faqs.decisionGuide: For safety-critical applications, verify isolation ratings meet your specific safety standard requirements.

faqs.question: What is the thermal derating for the LPS60-24?
faqs.answer: The LPS60-24 is rated for full 60W output from -25°C to +50°C ambient with natural convection cooling. Above 50°C, output power must be derated linearly to 50% at 70°C. The derating curve is based on the module's internal temperature rise and maximum allowable component temperatures. For example, at 60°C ambient, maximum output power is approximately 48W (80% of rated). The encapsulated package has better thermal performance than open-frame designs but still requires adequate airflow. For high-temperature applications, consider: Improving airflow around the module; Reducing load power; Selecting a higher-power module and operating at partial load; Mounting on a heatsink or metal chassis for improved heat dissipation. The module includes overtemperature protection that shuts down at approximately 105°C internal temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: For ambient temperatures above 50°C, calculate required derating or improve thermal management.

faqs.question: What external components are required for the LPS60-24?

Optional external components include: Inrush current limiter for multiple modules on one circuit; TVS diode for input surge protection; Output filter for noise-sensitive loads; Thermal fuse for additional safety. faqs.question: What is EN 50155 and why is it important for railway applications?
faqs.answer: EN 50155 is the European standard for electronic equipment used on rolling stock (trains, metros, trams). It specifies requirements for: Input voltage ranges including battery voltage variations and transients; Operating temperature ranges from -40°C to +70°C or higher; Shock and vibration resistance per EN 61373; EMC immunity for the electrically noisy railway environment; Fire safety per EN 45545-2. Compliance with EN 50155 ensures equipment will operate reliably in the harsh railway environment. Railway operators and system integrators typically require EN 50155 certification for all electronic equipment installed on rolling stock. P-Duke railway power supplies are fully tested and certified to meet these stringent requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For rolling stock applications, always select power supplies with full EN 50155 certification to ensure compliance and reliability.

faqs.question: What is the significance of 14:1 input range for railway applications?
faqs.answer: The 14:1 ultra-wide input range (14-160VDC) is critical for railway battery systems: Railway batteries are typically 24V, 48V, 72V, 96V, or 110V nominal; Battery voltage varies from discharged (0.7x nominal) to charging (1.25x nominal); A 14:1 converter handles all standard railway battery voltages; Eliminates need for different converters for different battery systems; Provides compatibility across different train types and regions. For example, a 110V battery system ranges from approximately 77V (discharged) to 137V (charging), while a 24V system ranges from 17V to 30V. The 14-160V range covers all these variations plus provides margin for voltage transients. This wide range simplifies inventory and maintenance for railway operators with mixed fleets.
faqs.decisionGuide: For railway applications with varying battery systems, select 14:1 input range converters for maximum compatibility.

faqs.question: What fire safety certifications do P-Duke railway supplies have?
faqs.answer: P-Duke railway power supplies meet EN 45545-2 fire safety requirements for rolling stock: HL3 (Hazard Level 3) rating for maximum fire safety; R22 and R23 requirements for electrical equipment; Low smoke emission during combustion; Halogen-free materials to reduce toxic gas release; Flame retardant properties to prevent fire spread. EN 45545-2 replaces national standards (DIN 5510, NFF 16-101, etc.) with a unified European standard. The HL3 rating is the highest hazard level, required for equipment installed in areas where evacuation may be difficult. P-Duke railway supplies use materials and construction techniques that meet these stringent fire safety requirements, ensuring compliance with modern railway safety standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: For rolling stock applications, verify fire safety ratings meet EN 45545-2 HL3 requirements for maximum safety compliance.

faqs.question: What is the shock and vibration rating for railway power supplies?
faqs.answer: P-Duke railway power supplies meet EN 61373 Category 1, Class B for shock and vibration: Random vibration: 5-150Hz at 1.0g RMS (body mounted) or 1.5g RMS (bogie mounted); Shock: 50g, 11ms half-sine pulse; Category 1 covers equipment mounted on railway vehicles; Class B covers equipment on the body of the vehicle. The robust construction includes: Reinforced component mounting; Conformal coating for environmental protection; Strengthened solder joints; Vibration-resistant connectors. These ratings ensure reliable operation in the severe mechanical environment of rolling stock, including wheel-rail impacts, curve negotiation, and braking forces. P-Duke railway supplies undergo vibration testing during qualification to verify compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For railway applications, verify shock and vibration ratings meet EN 61373 requirements for your specific mounting location.

faqs.question: What is the transient protection in railway power supplies?
faqs.answer: P-Duke railway power supplies include comprehensive transient protection per EN 50155: Input voltage interruptions: Maintain output during 10ms, 30ms, or 100ms interruptions depending on class; Surge protection: Withstand 1.4x nominal voltage for 1 second; Load dump: Handle voltage spikes up to 1.5x nominal; Brownout: Continue operation during voltage dips to 0.6x nominal. The protection circuits include: Input capacitors for ride-through during interruptions; TVS diodes for surge suppression; Active control circuits for voltage limiting; Isolation barriers for safety. These protections are critical for railway applications where: Circuit breakers open and close causing voltage interruptions; Battery switching creates transients; Regenerative braking generates voltage spikes. The power supplies are designed to continue normal operation through these transient conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For railway applications, verify transient protection meets the specific class requirements (S1, S2, S3) for your application.

faqs.question: What is 2xMOPP and why is it important for medical applications?
faqs.answer: 2xMOPP (2 Means of Patient Protection) is the highest level of isolation required by IEC 60601-1 for medical equipment: MOPP refers to isolation that protects patients from electrical shock; 2xMOPP means two independent protection systems are required; Required for patient-connected equipment (Type BF and CF); Isolation must withstand 4000V AC test voltage; Creepage and clearance distances are strictly specified. The 2xMOPP requirement ensures that even if one protection system fails, the patient remains protected. This is critical for: Devices that contact patients directly (ECG electrodes, infusion pumps); Equipment used in life-support applications; Home healthcare devices where medical supervision is limited. P-Duke medical power modules are designed and tested to provide 2xMOPP isolation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For patient-connected medical equipment, always select power modules with 2xMOPP isolation per IEC 60601-1.

faqs.question: What is the leakage current specification for medical power modules?
faqs.answer: P-Duke medical power modules have ultra-low leakage current for patient safety: Earth leakage current is less than 100uA at 264VAC; Patient leakage current is less than 2uA; These values meet the strictest requirements of IEC 60601-1; Leakage current is measured with specialized equipment per the standard. Low leakage current is achieved through: Optimized transformer design with minimal capacitive coupling; Shielding between primary and secondary; Careful PCB layout to minimize stray capacitance; High-quality insulation materials. For medical applications, low leakage current is critical because: Patient may be connected to earth through the device; High leakage current can cause microshock; Cardiac applications are especially sensitive to leakage currents. P-Duke medical modules are tested 100% for leakage current in production.
faqs.decisionGuide: For sensitive medical applications, verify leakage current meets the requirements for your device classification.

faqs.question: What is the difference between BF and CF type medical equipment?
faqs.answer: IEC 60601-1 defines three types of medical equipment based on patient contact: Type B (Body) - equipment with no patient contact or contact with earth-connected parts; Type BF (Body Floating) - equipment with patient contact but not directly connected to heart; Type CF (Cardiac Floating) - equipment with direct cardiac contact (highest safety requirements). The key differences are: Type CF requires lowest leakage current (<10uA); Type CF requires highest isolation (2xMOPP minimum); Type BF requires 1xMOPP or 2xMOPP depending on application; Type B has less stringent requirements. P-Duke medical power modules meet requirements for all types: 2xMOPP isolation suitable for Type CF; Low leakage current meets Type CF limits; Comprehensive safety certifications cover all application types. For specific applications, verify the required equipment type with your safety assessor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Determine your equipment type (B, BF, or CF) early in design to ensure power module selection meets requirements.

faqs.question: What safety certifications do P-Duke medical modules have?
faqs.answer: P-Duke medical power modules carry comprehensive safety certifications: IEC 60601-1 3rd Edition for medical electrical equipment; ANSI/AAMI ES60601-1 for US medical device compliance; CSA C22.2 No. 60601-1 for Canadian compliance; EN 60601-1 for European medical device compliance; CB scheme for international certification acceptance; MDD/MDR documentation for European medical device directive. The certification package includes: Type test reports from accredited laboratories; Risk management file per ISO 14971; Usability file per IEC 62366; Biocompatibility assessment if applicable; Clinical evaluation documentation. These certifications enable global deployment of medical devices using P-Duke power modules. For specific market requirements, P-Duke can provide additional documentation and support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify the specific medical safety certifications required for your target markets and device classification.

faqs.question: What is the isolation voltage for medical power modules?
faqs.answer: P-Duke medical power modules provide high isolation voltage for patient safety: Isolation test voltage is 5000V AC for 60 seconds; Working voltage is 250V AC continuous; 2xMOPP rating per IEC 60601-1; Isolation resistance exceeds 1000M ohms at 500V DC. The high isolation is achieved through: Reinforced insulation construction; Multiple insulation barriers; Adequate creepage and clearance distances; 100% production testing. For medical applications, high isolation is critical because: Patient may be connected to the device; Fault conditions must not expose patient to dangerous voltages; Isolation must be maintained over product lifetime. The 5000V test voltage provides substantial margin over the 4000V requirement for 2xMOPP, ensuring reliable isolation throughout the product life.
faqs.decisionGuide: For patient-connected equipment, verify isolation ratings meet 2xMOPP requirements per IEC 60601-1.

faqs.question: What is the output voltage accuracy?

faqs.question: What is the thermal derating curve?

faqs.question: What is the isolation capacitance?

The 25pF capacitance is very low for a 20W converter, reflecting the careful transformer design for medical applications. For comparison, typical industrial converters may have 100-500pF isolation capacitance. The low capacitance is one factor that enables the <2uA patient leakage current specification.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications sensitive to capacitive coupling, the low isolation capacitance minimizes leakage and noise coupling.

faqs.question: What is the mean time between failures (MTBF)?
faqs.answer: The MPS20-24S12 has high MTBF for reliable medical operation: MTBF is 1,600,000 hours at 25°C per MIL-HDBK-217F; At 40°C, MTBF is approximately 1,100,000 hours; At 50°C, MTBF is approximately 800,000 hours.
